testCases.title: The user should be able to register successfully when all required fields contain valid data
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
A valid unregistered email address is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration form is displayed with email and password fields visible

steps.content: Tap the email input field and enter a valid, unregistered email address (e.g., testuser_new@example.com)
steps.expected: Email is entered without error; email keyboard is displayed

steps.content: Tap the password input field and enter a valid password that meets complexity requirements (e.g., Test@1234)
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked; no inline error is shown

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button
steps.expected: A loading indicator is displayed while the request is processed

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: Registration succeeds; user receives a confirmation prompt or is navigated to the next step (e.g., email verification notice or home screen)

testCases.expectedResult: A new account is created successfully; the user receives a confirmation or next-step prompt without any error messages.

testCases.title: The registration form should display a confirmation prompt when a new account is created successfully
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Valid unregistered email and compliant password are ready
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Enter a valid unregistered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is accepted with no inline error

steps.content: Enter a valid password meeting complexity requirements in the password field
steps.expected: Password is accepted and masked

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button
steps.expected: Loading state is shown

steps.content: Observe the post-submission screen or message
steps.expected: A success confirmation message or next-step prompt (e.g., 'Check your email to verify your account') is displayed to the user

testCases.expectedResult: The user sees a clear confirmation or next-step prompt after successful registration, indicating the account was created.

testCases.title: The registration form should display a duplicate email error when an already-registered email is submitted
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
An already-registered email address is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the email input field and enter an email address that is already associated with an existing account
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap the password input field and enter a valid password meeting complexity requirements
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button
steps.expected: Loading state is shown briefly

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the email is already in use (e.g., 'An account with this email already exists'); registration does not proceed

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects the registration and displays an appropriate error message without creating a duplicate account.

testCases.title: The registration form should reject submission when an email already linked to a Google SSO account is used for email/password registration
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
An email address already registered via Google SSO is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Enter the email address that is already associated with a Google SSO account into the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Enter a valid password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button
steps.expected: Loading state is shown

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the email is already associated with a social login account; registration via email/password is blocked

testCases.expectedResult: The system prevents duplicate account creation and informs the user that the email is already linked to a Google SSO account.

testCases.title: The registration form should not submit a duplicate request when the user rapidly double-taps the submit button
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Valid unregistered email and compliant password are entered
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Enter a valid unregistered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is accepted

steps.content: Enter a valid password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is accepted and masked

steps.content: Rapidly double-tap the Submit / Register button in quick succession
steps.expected: Only one submission request is sent; the button is disabled or debounced after the first tap to prevent duplicate requests

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: Only one account is created; no duplicate account or duplicate API call is triggered

testCases.expectedResult: The system prevents duplicate submissions; only one account creation request is processed regardless of rapid tapping.

testCases.title: The registration form should display an error when the registration API is unavailable
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Valid unregistered email and compliant password are entered
The registration API is simulated to be unavailable (5xx error or network timeout)

steps.content: Enter a valid unregistered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is accepted

steps.content: Enter a valid password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is accepted and masked

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button while the API is unavailable
steps.expected: Loading state is shown briefly

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: A meaningful error message is displayed (e.g., 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'); the form remains intact so the user can retry

testCases.expectedResult: The system handles the API failure gracefully and displays a user-friendly error without crashing or losing the user's input.

testCases.title: The registration form should handle maximum-length input without crashing when email or password exceeds typical field length limits
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the email field and enter an email address exceeding 255 characters (e.g., a string of 250 'a' characters followed by '@test.com')
steps.expected: The field either caps input at the maximum allowed length or accepts the input for validation

steps.content: Tap the password field and enter a password string exceeding 255 characters
steps.expected: The field either caps input at the maximum allowed length or accepts the input for validation

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button
steps.expected: The app does not crash; an appropriate validation error is displayed if the input exceeds allowed limits

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles oversized inputs gracefully without crashing, freezing, or producing unexpected behavior.

testCases.title: The registration form should display an error when the device has no internet connectivity at the time of submission
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
Valid email and password are entered
Device is in airplane mode or has no network connectivity

steps.content: Enable airplane mode or disable network connectivity on the device
steps.expected: Device has no internet connection

steps.content: Enter a valid email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered

steps.content: Enter a valid password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Register button
steps.expected: A meaningful offline or connectivity error message is displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection. Please check your network and try again.'); the form is not cleared

testCases.expectedResult: The system displays a clear offline error message and does not crash or silently fail when there is no network connectivity.

testCases.title: The user should be redirected to Google OAuth when the Sign in with Google button is tapped
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Network connectivity is active
A dedicated Google test account is available

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with a 'Sign in with Google' option visible

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: The native Google Sign-In flow is initiated; the user is redirected to the Google authentication screen or a Google account selector is displayed

testCases.expectedResult: Tapping 'Sign in with Google' successfully initiates the Google OAuth flow and presents the Google authentication UI.

testCases.title: The user should be logged in successfully when Google OAuth is completed and the account already exists
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A Google account already linked to an existing app account is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button on the Login screen
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Select or authenticate with the Google test account that is already registered in the app
steps.expected: Google authentication completes successfully

steps.content: Wait for the app to process the OAuth response
steps.expected: The user is returned to the app and logged in; a valid session is created; the user is navigated to the home/dashboard screen

testCases.expectedResult: The user is successfully authenticated via Google SSO and a valid session is established within the app.

testCases.title: The system should create a new account when Google SSO is used for the first time with an unregistered email
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A Google account whose email is NOT registered in the app is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button on the Login screen
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Authenticate with a Google account whose email is not yet registered in the app
steps.expected: Google authentication completes successfully

steps.content: Wait for the app to process the OAuth response
steps.expected: The system creates a new account using the Google profile data; the user is logged in and navigated to the home/dashboard screen or an onboarding step

testCases.expectedResult: A new account is automatically created for the unregistered Google email and the user is logged in successfully.

testCases.title: The login flow should fail gracefully when the user cancels the Google OAuth flow
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button on the Login screen
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Cancel or dismiss the Google authentication screen (e.g., tap the back button or cancel option)
steps.expected: The Google OAuth screen is dismissed

steps.content: Observe the app state
steps.expected: The user is returned to the Login screen without being logged in; no error crash occurs; an optional informational message may be shown (e.g., 'Sign-in was cancelled')

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles OAuth cancellation gracefully and returns the user to the Login screen without crashing or leaving the user in an inconsistent state.

testCases.title: The app should return the user to the login screen when the Google OAuth flow is interrupted by a phone call or app backgrounding
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User has initiated the Google OAuth flow
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button to initiate the OAuth flow
steps.expected: Google OAuth screen is displayed

steps.content: Simulate an interruption by receiving a phone call or pressing the Home button to background the app during the OAuth flow
steps.expected: The app is backgrounded or interrupted

steps.content: Return to the app after the interruption
steps.expected: The app resumes gracefully; the user is returned to the Login screen or the OAuth flow resumes without crashing or leaving the user in a broken state

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles mid-flow interruptions gracefully and does not crash, freeze, or leave the user in an inconsistent authentication state.

testCases.title: The system should handle token exchange failure gracefully when the Google OAuth token cannot be exchanged due to a network timeout
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Network connectivity is degraded or simulated to timeout during token exchange

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Complete Google authentication on the Google screen
steps.expected: Google authentication is completed on the Google side

steps.content: Simulate a network timeout during the token exchange step (e.g., disable network just after Google auth completes)
steps.expected: The token exchange request times out

steps.content: Observe the app response
steps.expected: A meaningful error message is displayed (e.g., 'Sign-in failed. Please try again.'); the user is returned to the Login screen without being partially logged in

testCases.expectedResult: The system handles OAuth token exchange failures gracefully and presents a clear error without leaving the user in a broken session state.

testCases.title: The login should succeed when the user has previously revoked and then re-granted app permissions in their Google account
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
The test Google account has previously revoked app permissions and then re-granted them
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: Google OAuth flow is initiated

steps.content: Select the Google account that previously revoked and re-granted permissions
steps.expected: Google presents the permission grant screen or proceeds directly if permissions are already re-granted

steps.content: Grant the required permissions if prompted
steps.expected: Permissions are granted

steps.content: Wait for the app to process the OAuth response
steps.expected: Login succeeds; the user is navigated to the home/dashboard screen with a valid session

testCases.expectedResult: The system successfully handles re-granted permissions and completes the login flow without errors.

testCases.title: The forgot password form should accept a valid registered email and trigger a reset email when submitted
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A registered email address with accessible inbox is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Tap the 'Forgot Password' link on the Login screen
steps.expected: The Forgot Password screen is displayed with an email input field

steps.content: Enter a valid registered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered without inline error

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Send Reset Email button
steps.expected: Loading state is shown

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: A generic confirmation message is displayed (e.g., 'If this email is registered, you will receive a password reset link shortly')

steps.content: Check the registered email inbox
steps.expected: A password reset email is received containing a reset link or token

testCases.expectedResult: The system sends a password reset email to the registered address and displays a confirmation message to the user.

testCases.title: The user should be able to set a new password when the reset link is valid and unexpired
preconditions: A valid, unexpired password reset link has been received in the test email inbox
App is installed on a mobile device
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Open the password reset email and tap the reset link
steps.expected: The app opens (or a browser opens) and navigates to the password reset screen with a new password input field

steps.content: Enter a new valid password that meets complexity requirements in the new password field
steps.expected: New password is entered and masked

steps.content: Confirm the new password in the confirm password field (if present)
steps.expected: Confirm password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Reset Password button
steps.expected: Loading state is shown

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: A success message is displayed (e.g., 'Your password has been reset successfully'); the user is redirected to the Login screen

steps.content: Attempt to log in with the new password
steps.expected: Login succeeds with the new password

testCases.expectedResult: The user successfully resets their password using a valid reset link and can log in with the new credentials.

testCases.title: The forgot password form should display a generic confirmation message when a non-registered email is submitted to prevent account enumeration
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Forgot Password screen
An email address that is NOT registered in the system is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Enter an email address that is not registered in the system into the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered without inline format error

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Send Reset Email button
steps.expected: Loading state is shown

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: The same generic confirmation message is displayed as for a registered email (e.g., 'If this email is registered, you will receive a password reset link shortly'); no indication is given as to whether the email exists in the system

testCases.expectedResult: The system displays a non-revealing generic message for unregistered emails, preventing account enumeration attacks.

testCases.title: The reset link should be rejected when it is used a second time after a successful password change
preconditions: A password reset has already been completed using a specific reset link
The used reset link/token is available for re-use attempt
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Attempt to open the already-used password reset link again (e.g., tap the link in the original reset email a second time)
steps.expected: The app or browser navigates to the reset password screen or an error page

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the link is invalid or has already been used (e.g., 'This reset link has already been used or has expired'); the user cannot set a new password using this link

testCases.expectedResult: The system invalidates the reset token after first use and rejects any subsequent attempts to use the same link.

testCases.title: The reset link should be rejected when it is accessed after the expiry window has passed
preconditions: An expired password reset link/token is available (pre-generated or time-advanced)
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Open the expired password reset link
steps.expected: The app or browser navigates to the reset password screen or an error page

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the link has expired (e.g., 'This reset link has expired. Please request a new one.'); the user cannot set a new password

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects expired reset tokens and informs the user to request a new reset link.

testCases.title: The user should be able to request a new reset email when the original reset link has expired
preconditions: User has received an expired reset link
App is launched on a mobile device
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Attempt to use the expired reset link and observe the expiry error message
steps.expected: Expiry error is displayed with an option or instruction to request a new reset link

steps.content: Navigate back to the Forgot Password screen (via the error page link or manually)
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter the registered email address and tap Submit
steps.expected: Loading state is shown

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: A new confirmation message is displayed; a new reset email is sent to the inbox

steps.content: Check the email inbox for the new reset email
steps.expected: A new, valid reset link is received

testCases.expectedResult: The user can successfully request and receive a new password reset email after the original link has expired.

testCases.title: The system should apply rate limiting when rapid successive reset requests are made from the same email address
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Forgot Password screen
A registered email address is available
Network connectivity is active

steps.content: Enter a registered email address in the Forgot Password email field
steps.expected: Email is entered

steps.content: Tap the Submit button multiple times in rapid succession (e.g., 5 or more times within a short window)
steps.expected: The first request is processed; subsequent rapid requests are throttled or blocked

steps.content: Observe the system response after multiple submissions
steps.expected: The system either disables the submit button after the first request, displays a rate-limit message (e.g., 'Please wait before requesting another reset email'), or limits the number of emails sent

steps.content: Check the email inbox
steps.expected: Only a limited number of reset emails are received (not an unlimited flood of emails)

testCases.expectedResult: The system enforces rate limiting on password reset requests to prevent email flooding and abuse.

testCases.title: The email field should display an inline error when the user enters an email without the @ symbol
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen

steps.content: Tap the email input field
steps.expected: Email field is focused; email keyboard is displayed

steps.content: Enter an email address missing the @ symbol (e.g., 'userdomain.com')
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap outside the field or tap the Submit button to trigger validation
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed below the email field indicating an invalid email format (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address')

testCases.expectedResult: The system displays an inline validation error for an email missing the @ symbol and prevents form submission.

testCases.title: The email field should display an inline error when the user enters an email with only the @ symbol and no domain
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen

steps.content: Tap the email input field
steps.expected: Email field is focused

steps.content: Enter 'user@' (missing domain) in the email field
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap outside the field or tap the Submit button
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating an invalid email format

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects 'user@' as an invalid email format and displays an appropriate inline error.

testCases.title: The email field should display an inline error when the user enters an email with only the domain and no local part
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen

steps.content: Tap the email input field
steps.expected: Email field is focused

steps.content: Enter '@domain.com' (missing local part) in the email field
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap outside the field or tap the Submit button
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating an invalid email format

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects '@domain.com' as an invalid email format and displays an appropriate inline error.

testCases.title: The email field should display an inline error when the user enters an email with a space in the address
preconditions: App is launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration or Login screen

steps.content: Tap the email input field
steps.expected: Email field is focused

steps.content: Enter 'test @test.com' (with a space) in the email field
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap outside the field or tap the Submit button
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating an invalid email format

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects an email address containing a space and displays an appropriate inline error.
